identity.name: SSH Outbound App-ID + Atypical Client Banner — C2 / Tunnel Setup (T1572 / T1021.004)
identity.summary: A non-admin endpoint repeatedly opens short-lived outbound SSH sessions to a host outside the corporate range, advertising an atypical SSH-2.0 client banner and exchanging KEXINIT before disconnecting. The CortexSim ssh_egress EAL plugin reproduces the protocol shape so NGFW App-ID identifies the session as ssh and XSIAM Network Security Analytics flags the atypical banner string plus the repea…
identity.description: SSH is a favored covert channel for both interactive C2 and tunneling (local/remote/dynamic port forwarding) because it is encrypted, ubiquitous and often allowed outbound. The pre-tunnel reconnaissance and connection-setup signature on the wire is distinctive even before any payload flows: an outbound session that NGFW App-ID resolves to ssh from a non-admin source toward a non-corporate destination; a non-standard SSH-2.0 client banner (custom client identification strings are a strong indicator of bespoke tooling rather than OpenSSH/PuTTY); and a burst of repeated short-lived handshakes (banner + KEXINIT then disconnect) from one source as the operator probes connectivity or rotates sessions. This card detects all three from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story: an App-ID validation query, an atypical-client-banner IOC, and a behavioural BIOC for the repeated short-lived handshake burst. A correlation rule fuses the App-ID, the banner IOC, and the handshake-burst BIOC into a single SSH-tunnel/C2-setup incident so the SOC sees one story rather than a stream of brief sessions.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1572 / T1021.004 — CortexSim ssh_egress EAL plugin (outbound SSH-2.0 banner + KEXINIT to lab sinkhole)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-007 requirements (NGFW logging outbound traffic from the lab subnet to XSIAM, TCP listener on 10.50.0.42:22).
NGFW forwards outbound traffic logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story carries app, src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, session duration).

payload.code:
# Live outbound SSH-2.0 banner + KEXINIT handshake burst against lab sinkhole.
cat > /tmp/ssh-egress-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-007
name: "NDR validation — SSH outbound App-ID + banner anomaly"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - 10.50.0.0/24
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, ssh, eal, banner, c2]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: ssh_egress
    params:
      target_host: "10.50.0.42"
      target_port: 22
      client_banner: "SSH-2.0-Cortex_atypical_lateral_001"
      iterations: 5
      sleep_seconds: 12
      send_kexinit: true
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/ssh-egress-live.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound TCP session on port 22 identified by NGFW App-ID as ssh toward a non-corporate destination.
Atypical SSH-2.0 client identification banner advertised on connect.
Repeated short-lived SSH handshakes (banner + KEXINIT then disconnect) from a single source.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: app = "ssh" AND count() per src_ip >= 4 AND session_duration short

iocs.context: NGFW EAL — atypical SSH client banner observed (SSH-2.0-Cortex_atypical_lateral_001). Non-standard SSH-2.0 client identification string advertised on the outbound SSH session, indicative of bespoke tooling rather than a sanctioned SSH client.

biocs.name: NGFW EAL repeated short-lived outbound SSH handshakes from single source
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — repeated short-lived outbound SSH handshakes from single source
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app = "ssh" or dst_port = 22
| filter dst_ip != null and not incidr(dst_ip, "10.0.0.0/8")
| filter action_session_duration <= 5
| comp count() as handshake_count, count_distinct(dst_ip) as dst_hosts by src_ip, dst_port
| filter handshake_count >= 4
| fields src_ip, dst_port, handshake_count, dst_hosts

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight SSH sinkhole reachable on port 22
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 22
| filter dst_ip = "10.50.0.42"
| comp count() as ssh_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, ssh_sessions

xql_queries.name: NGFW App-ID SSH outbound session from non-admin endpoint
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app = "ssh" or dst_port = 22
| filter not incidr(dst_ip, "10.0.0.0/8") or dst_ip = "10.50.0.42"
| comp count() as ssh_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app
| filter ssh_sessions >= 1
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app, ssh_sessions

xql_queries.name: XSIAM correlation SSH banner anomaly plus handshake burst stitched
xql_queries.query:
preset = network_story
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app = "ssh" or dst_port = 22
| comp count() as handshakes, values(ssh_client_banner) as banners, avg(action_session_duration) as avg_dur by src_ip, dst_ip
| filter handshakes >= 4
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, handshakes, banners, avg_dur

correlation_rules.name: SSH Outbound Tunnel Setup — Atypical Banner Plus Handshake Burst
correlation_rules.logic: IOC(SSH-2.0-Cortex_atypical_lateral_001) OR BIOC(NGFW EAL repeated short-lived outbound SSH handshakes from single source) OR XQL(NGFW App-ID SSH outbound session from non-admin endpoint)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — Outbound SSH Tunnel / C2 Setup
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Atypical SSH Client Banner and Handshake Burst

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app = "ssh" | comp count() as ssh_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip
preset = network_story | filter app = "ssh" | comp count() as handshakes by src_ip, dst_ip | filter handshakes >= 4
Playbook: block the outbound SSH destination, quarantine the source host, hunt for established tunnels/port-forwards, open a C2 DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: Outbound SSH Tunnel / C2 Setup Detection via NGFW EAL
use_cases.description: Validate that NGFW App-ID + XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect outbound SSH with an atypical client banner and a repeated short-lived handshake burst from a non-admin endpoint.

test_cases.objective: Outbound SSH App-ID and the atypical client banner are detected from NGFW telemetry.
preconditions: NGFW logging outbound traffic from the lab subnet to XSIAM.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-007 step-02 (live ssh_egress with atypical banner + KEXINIT).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW App-ID SSH outbound session from non-admin endpoint` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: The handshake burst and banner anomaly stitch into a single tunnel/C2-setup incident.
success_criteria: BIOC `NGFW EAL repeated short-lived outbound SSH handshakes from single source` returns >= 1 row with handshake_count >= 4.
Correlation `CR-NDR-0006` raises a single SSH-tunnel/C2 incident for the source.

preventive_controls: Restrict outbound SSH at the NGFW to approved bastion/jump hosts and sanctioned destinations only.
Apply default-deny outbound policy and block ssh App-ID from workstation subnets.
Enforce SSH client allowlisting so only sanctioned clients/banners are permitted.
detection_engineering: Alert on ssh App-ID from non-admin endpoints toward non-corporate destinations.
Surface atypical/non-standard SSH client identification banners.
Correlate repeated short-lived SSH handshakes with the banner anomaly into one tunnel/C2-setup incident.

references.title: Unit42 - SSH Tunneling for C2 and Lateral Movement
